description: Simple rectangle drawn with relative h/v/z commands
reason_correct: H/V/Z relative commands expand to absolute endpoints spanning the declared width and height

description: Two subpaths with relative h/v/z; bbox must be the union of both
reason_correct: Second subpath starts at (60,70) via relative m; union bbox spans both rectangles

description: Path mixing absolute L/V with relative h/v
reason_correct: Relative h 10 from x=15 reaches x=25; relative v 5 from y=30 reaches y=35

description: Semicircle A 50 50 0 0 0 100 0 -- bbox must include y=50 bulge not just endpoints
reason_correct: sweep=0 CCW arc from (0,0) to (100,0) bulges downward to y=50; endpoint-only code would give height=0

description: Semicircle with sweep=1 CW arcs upward to y=-50
reason_correct: sweep=1 CW arc bulges upward to y=-50 (negative y in SVG coords is upward)

description: Quarter-circle arc from (50,0) to (100,50) with rx=ry=50
reason_correct: Quarter arc stays within the bounding box of its two endpoints for this geometry

description: Simple clipPath fully containing the target; WP-3e flattens the clip to the target geometry and removes the clipPath def, so the bbox is the visible clipped region (the 10x10 target), not the defs-inclusive box
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="a"><path d="M-.08.08h136.877v393.601H-.08z" /></clipPath></defs><path clip-path="url(#a)" d="M 5 5 h 10 v 10 z" fill="#000" />
reason_correct: The clip rect (-0.08..136.797, 0.08..393.681) fully contains the 10x10 target rect at (5,5); the intersection equals the target, the clipPath def is dropped, and the bbox is the visible clipped region (5,5,15,15)

description: Rectangle at offset (10,20) produces viewBox 0 0 W H after crop+shift with padding=2
svg_body: <rect x="10" y="20" width="30" height="40" fill="#000" />
reason_correct: Rect bbox is the declared x/y/width/height; after normalization viewBox shifts to 0 0 34 44 (adding 2px padding each side)

description: Rect element bbox computed directly without path conversion
svg_body: <rect x="5" y="10" width="20" height="15" fill="#333" />
reason_correct: element_bbox for rect returns BBox(x, y, x+w, y+h) directly from attributes

description: Circle element bbox: cx=50 cy=50 r=30
reason_correct: element_bbox for circle is BBox(cx-r, cy-r, cx+r, cy+r)

description: Line element bbox from endpoints including stroke pad (A3: stroke-width=1 gives pad=2)
reason_correct: element_bbox pads geometry BBox(10,20,40,60) by stroke_width/2*miterlimit=1/2*4=2 on each side (A3 stroke-pad)

description: Truncated SVG must reject with PARSER_ERROR and write no output
reason_correct: Malformed XML (unclosed tag) must trigger PARSER_ERROR rejection; no output file written

description: SVG with no drawable shapes must reject with EMPTY_GEOMETRY
svg_body: <defs></defs>
reason_correct: An SVG with only defs and no visible shapes has no drawable geometry; EMPTY_GEOMETRY rejection

description: Rect inside a group with translate(5,7); transform flattens into coords (WP-2a)
svg_body: <g transform="translate(5,7)"><rect x="10" y="10" width="20" height="20" fill="#000"/></g>
reason_correct: translate(5,7) shifts the rect 10..30 in both axes to 15..35 (x) and 17..37 (y) in root coords

description: Rect with scale(2,3) applied directly; non-uniform scale flattens into coords
svg_body: <rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10" transform="scale(2,3)" fill="#000"/>
reason_correct: scale(2,3) maps the 10x10 rect to 20 wide and 30 tall in root coords (fill-only, no stroke, so non-uniform scale is allowed)

description: Path with rotate(90) inside translate(100,0) group; rotation+translate chain
svg_body: <g transform="translate(100,0)"><path d="M 0 0 L 10 0 L 10 10" transform="rotate(90)" fill="#000"/></g>
reason_correct: rotate(90) maps (x,y)->(-y,x): the L-shaped path spans x in [-10,0], y in [0,10]; translate(100,0) shifts x to [90,100]

description: Rect with matrix(-1,0,0,1,20,0): a horizontal flip about x=10
svg_body: <rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10" transform="matrix(-1,0,0,1,20,0)" fill="#000"/>
reason_correct: matrix(-1,0,0,1,20,0) maps x->20-x, so the rect 0..10 becomes 10..20; y is unchanged

description: Rect under translate(10,10) > scale(2): nested-group transform chain composes
svg_body: <g transform="translate(10,10)"><g transform="scale(2)"><rect x="0" y="0" width="5" height="5" fill="#000"/></g></g>
reason_correct: scale(2) maps the 5x5 rect to 10x10, then translate(10,10) shifts it to 10..20 in both axes (outermost-to-element composition order)

description: Half-ellipse arc rotated 90 degrees; arc-under-matrix uses the SVD path
svg_body: <path d="M 0 0 A 50 30 0 0 0 100 0" transform="rotate(90)" fill="#000"/>
reason_correct: The down-bulging half-ellipse spans x in [0,100] and y in [0,30]; rotate(90) maps (x,y)->(-y,x), giving x in [-30,0] and y in [0,100]

description: Sharp rect (no rx/ry) converts to path; bbox matches declared geometry (WP-2b A2)
svg_body: <rect x="10" y="20" width="60" height="40" fill="#000"/>
reason_correct: Sharp rect path M/H/V/H/z spans exactly the declared x/y/width/height; bbox equals (x, y, x+w, y+h)

description: Rounded rect with rx=10 only; ry defaults to rx=10; corners are quarter-arcs (WP-2b A2)
svg_body: <rect x="0" y="0" width="60" height="40" rx="10" fill="#000"/>
reason_correct: Rounded rect bbox equals the outer rectangle; arcs stay within (x,y,x+w,y+h). rx=10 ry=10 (clamped to min(10,w/2)=10 and min(10,h/2)=10)

description: Rounded rect with rx=8 ry=5; distinct horizontal/vertical radii (WP-2b A2)
svg_body: <rect x="10" y="5" width="40" height="30" rx="8" ry="5" fill="#000"/>
reason_correct: Rounded rect with distinct rx and ry; outer bbox equals (x,y,x+w,y+h); quarter-arcs stay within bounds

description: Circle cx=50 cy=50 r=30 converts to two-arc path; bbox matches (WP-2b A2)
reason_correct: Circle path uses two half-arc segments; bbox is (cx-r, cy-r, cx+r, cy+r) = (20,20,80,80)

description: Ellipse cx=30 cy=40 rx=20 ry=10 converts to two-arc path (WP-2b A2)
reason_correct: Ellipse path uses two half-arcs (rx=20 ry=10); bbox is (cx-rx, cy-ry, cx+rx, cy+ry) = (10,30,50,50)

description: Line x1=5 y1=10 x2=80 y2=60 converts to M/L path; bbox includes stroke pad (A3)
reason_correct: Line geometry (5,10,80,60) padded by stroke_width/2*miterlimit=1/2*4=2 on each side (A3 stroke-pad)

description: Polyline with three points converts to M/L/L path; bbox includes stroke pad (A3)
svg_body: <polyline points="0,0 50,20 100,0" fill="none" stroke="#000"/>
reason_correct: Polyline geometry (0,0,100,20) padded by stroke_width/2*miterlimit=1/2*4=2 (stroke-width defaults to 1) (A3 stroke-pad)

description: Polygon triangle converts to M/L/L/Z path (WP-2b A2)
reason_correct: Polygon path M 10 10 L 90 10 L 50 80 Z; bbox spans all three vertices

description: Thick-stroke horizontal path: padded bbox larger than geometry bbox by stroke_width/2*miterlimit (A3)
svg_body: <path d="M 10 10 L 90 10" stroke="#000" stroke-width="10" fill="none"/>
reason_correct: Geometry bbox (10,10,90,10). Butt linecap (default): pad = stroke_width/2*max(1,miterlimit) = 10/2*4 = 20 per side. Padded bbox = (-10,-10,110,30). Width=120, height=40; with padding=2 viewBox is 0 0 124 44.

description: A stroked path under scale(2) has its stroke-width baked-scaled, so the stroke-padded bbox uses the SCALED stroke (the cpu.svg mesh-greyout fix)
svg_body: <path d="M 0 0 L 10 0" transform="scale(2)" stroke="#000" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/>
reason_correct: scale(2) maps the line (0,0)-(10,0) to (0,0)-(20,0) and scales stroke-width 2 -> 4. Butt linecap pad = stroke_width/2*miterlimit = 4/2*4 = 8 per side, giving bbox (-8,-8,28,8). Without stroke-width scaling the pad would be wrong (4 per side).

description: SVG with a <text> element must be rejected with TEXT_UNSUPPORTED (A5)
svg_body: <rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="100" fill="#000"/><text x="10" y="50">Hello</text>
reason_correct: Text elements cannot be normalized by v3; authors must convert text to paths before ingestion

description: SVG with a <use> element must be rejected (WP-3b)
svg_body: <defs><rect id="r" x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10"/></defs><use href="#r" x="20" y="20"/>
reason_correct: Symbol/use expansion is not implemented in v3; refuse the whole file

description: SVG with a <filter>/feGaussianBlur must be rejected (WP-3b)
svg_body: <defs><filter id="f"><feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="2"/></filter></defs><rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10" fill="#000" filter="url(#f)"/>
reason_correct: Filters can extend visible pixels beyond the geometry bbox (contract item 3)

description: SVG with a <mask> must be rejected (WP-3b)
svg_body: <defs><mask id="m"><rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10" fill="#fff"/></mask></defs><rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10" fill="#000" mask="url(#m)"/>
reason_correct: Masks change visible pixels in ways not computable from geometry

description: SVG with a marker reference must be rejected (WP-3b)
svg_body: <defs><marker id="mk"><path d="M 0 0 L 5 5 z"/></marker></defs><path d="M 0 0 L 10 0" stroke="#000" marker-end="url(#mk)"/>
reason_correct: Markers draw extra geometry the bbox cannot account for

description: SVG with an <image> data: URI must be rejected (WP-3b)
svg_body: <image x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10" href="data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUg=="/>
reason_correct: An embedded base64 raster is not vector geometry v3 can normalize

description: SVG with an <image> external href must be rejected (WP-3b)
svg_body: <image x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10" href="https://example.com/x.png"/>
reason_correct: An external resource reference is not self-contained vector geometry

description: A clipPath holding more than one child shape is outside the simple allowlist and must be rejected as complex (WP-3e)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10"/><rect x="20" y="20" width="10" height="10"/></clipPath></defs><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="40" fill="#000" clip-path="url(#c)"/>
reason_correct: The simple-clip allowlist requires exactly one path/shape inside the clipPath; two children make the clip complex

description: SVG with a geometry-affecting <style> rule must be rejected (WP-3b)
svg_body: <style>rect { fill: red; stroke-width: 4px; }</style><rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10"/>
reason_correct: v3 resolves geometry from inline style only; a stylesheet geometry rule is unsupported

description: SVG with an unparseable <style> block must be rejected (WP-3b)
svg_body: <style>}}}</style><rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10" fill="#000"/>
reason_correct: tinycss2 reports a stylesheet error node for this malformed CSS

description: SVG with a pattern that has child geometry must be rejected (WP-3b)
svg_body: <defs><pattern id="p" width="4" height="4"><rect x="0" y="0" width="2" height="2"/></pattern></defs><rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10" fill="#000"/>
reason_correct: A pattern with child geometry would need a pattern renderer to bbox; reject

description: SVG with a url(#) reference to an undefined id must be rejected (S1, WP-3b)
svg_body: <rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10" fill="url(#nope)"/>
reason_correct: S1 reference-integrity gate rejects a dangling internal reference

description: Rect target clipped by a rect clipPath; result is the overlap region (WP-3e A6)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><rect x="5" y="5" width="40" height="40"/></clipPath></defs><rect x="20" y="20" width="60" height="60" fill="#000" clip-path="url(#c)"/>
reason_correct: Target rect (20..80) intersected with clip rect (5..45) yields the overlap (20..45) in both axes

description: Rect target clipped by a circle clipPath; result fits inside the circle (WP-3e A6)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><circle cx="50" cy="50" r="30"/></clipPath></defs><rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="100" fill="#000" clip-path="url(#c)"/>
reason_correct: The clipped region is the circle (cx=50, cy=50, r=30) since it lies entirely inside the target rect; bbox spans 20..80 within the curve-flattening tolerance

description: Rect target clipped by a triangle polygon clipPath (WP-3e A6)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><polygon points="10,10 90,10 50,90"/></clipPath></defs><rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="100" fill="#000" clip-path="url(#c)"/>
reason_correct: The triangle clip lies inside the target rect; the clipped region is the triangle spanning x 10..90, y 10..90

description: Curved (cubic bezier) target clipped by a left-half rect; curves flatten to polylines before intersection (WP-3e A6)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><rect x="0" y="0" width="50" height="200"/></clipPath></defs><path d="M 10 100 C 10 10 190 10 190 100 C 190 190 10 190 10 100 Z" fill="#000" clip-path="url(#c)"/>
reason_correct: The lens-shaped cubic blob spans x 10..190; clipping to x<=50 keeps the left lobe (x 10..50). The y extent of that lobe is bounded by the bezier, ~42.8..157.2 within curve-flattening tolerance

description: Transformed target (group translate) clipped by a rect; transform flattens first, then the clip (WP-3e A6)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="100"/></clipPath></defs><g transform="translate(20,20)"><rect x="0" y="0" width="60" height="60" fill="#000" clip-path="url(#c)"/></g>
reason_correct: translate(20,20) moves the 60x60 target to 20..80 in root coords; the 0..100 clip fully contains it so the clipped region is the whole target (20..80)

description: Clip child carries its own transform; flattening composes it into root coords before intersection (WP-3e A6)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="40" transform="translate(30,30)"/></clipPath></defs><rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="100" fill="#000" clip-path="url(#c)"/>
reason_correct: The clip rect with translate(30,30) occupies 30..70 in root coords; intersected with the 0..100 target it yields the clip region 30..70

description: Disjoint target and clip yield an empty intersection; with no other geometry the document is EMPTY_GEOMETRY (WP-3e A6)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><rect x="0" y="0" width="10" height="10"/></clipPath></defs><rect x="100" y="100" width="20" height="20" fill="#000" clip-path="url(#c)"/>
reason_correct: The target (100..120) and clip (0..10) do not overlap, so the clipped element has no geometry; it is the only drawable element, so the document has no drawable geometry (EMPTY_GEOMETRY)

description: A line clip child has no fillable area; the intersection is empty and (sole geometry) rejects EMPTY_GEOMETRY (WP-3e A6)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><line x1="0" y1="0" x2="100" y2="100"/></clipPath></defs><rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="100" fill="#000" clip-path="url(#c)"/>
reason_correct: A line clip child encloses no area, so the filled-area intersection with the target is empty; with no other geometry the document is EMPTY_GEOMETRY

description: Stroke-only (fill:none) target fully inside a containing rect clip; the no-op short circuit drops the clip ref and keeps the target geometry (Rank 1 no-op clip elimination)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><path d="M 10 10 H 90 V 90 H 10 Z"/></clipPath></defs><path clip-path="url(#c)" d="M 30 30 L 60 30 L 60 60 Z" fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1"/>
reason_correct: The stroke envelope of the triangle (geometry 30..60) lies inside the 10..90 clip, so the clip is a no-op: the ref is dropped and the original geometry kept. bbox is the geometry (30,30,60,60) padded by stroke_width/2*miterlimit=1/2*4=2 to (28,28,62,62)

description: Stroke-only target genuinely trimmed by a clip that cuts across the stroke; a real stroke trim stays rejected as complex (out of scope)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><path d="M 0 0 H 45 V 100 H 0 Z"/></clipPath></defs><path clip-path="url(#c)" d="M 30 30 L 60 30 L 60 60 Z" fill="none" stroke="#000" stroke-width="1"/>
reason_correct: The triangle stroke spans x 30..60 but the clip keeps only x<=45, so the clip genuinely cuts the stroke (not a no-op). A true stroke trim needs stroke-to-path expansion which is out of scope; the stroke-only target stays rejected

description: Filled target fully inside its clip; the no-op short circuit drops the ref and keeps the original d instead of re-emitting a polygonized intersection (no precision loss)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><path d="M 10 10 H 90 V 90 H 10 Z"/></clipPath></defs><path clip-path="url(#c)" d="M 30 30 L 60 30 L 60 60 Z" fill="#000"/>
reason_correct: The filled triangle (30..60) lies inside the 10..90 clip, so the clip is a no-op: ref dropped, original geometry kept. bbox is the triangle bbox (30,30,60,60) with no stroke pad

description: Filled target partially clipped; the clip is not a no-op so the existing intersection path runs and the bbox shrinks to the overlap (existing behavior preserved)
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><path d="M 0 0 H 45 V 100 H 0 Z"/></clipPath></defs><path clip-path="url(#c)" d="M 20 20 L 80 20 L 80 80 L 20 80 Z" fill="#000"/>
reason_correct: The filled rect (20..80) pokes past the clip's x<=45, so the clip genuinely trims it; the intersection is x 20..45, y 20..80

description: A clip with two child shapes (complex clip side) over a contained target; clip-side complexity wins and the file is rejected even though the target would otherwise be a no-op
svg_body: <defs><clipPath id="c"><rect x="0" y="0" width="100" height="100"/><rect x="10" y="10" width="5" height="5"/></clipPath></defs><path clip-path="url(#c)" d="M 30 30 L 60 30 L 60 60 Z" fill="#000"/>
reason_correct: The clipPath holds two child shapes, which fails the clip-side allowlist (exactly one geometry child). The clip-polygon build raises before the no-op check, so the file is rejected as complex even though the 100x100 clip would contain the target
